assertions.expectation: First step: check the operation status to understand why it failed (gcloud container operations list)
assertions.reason: Response includes 'gcloud container operations list --filter="TYPE:UPGRADE_NODES"' command to check operation status and get error details

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking events and logs for the specific failure reason
assertions.reason: Response recommends getting specific error details from operations list and mentions reviewing the error from GCP Console or logs

assertions.expectation: Explains that the cluster is in a mixed-version state (8 nodes at 1.32, 12 at 1.31) which is within skew policy
assertions.reason: While the response acknowledges mixed-version nodes exist, it doesn't explicitly explain the version skew policy or confirm this specific state is within policy

assertions.expectation: Notes that the mixed state is functional but not ideal — workloads can run on both versions
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking workload health on mixed-version nodes and implies functionality, though doesn't explicitly state 'not ideal'

assertions.expectation: Provides options: retry the upgrade after fixing the root cause, or rollback the 8 upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: Response provides retry options but does not mention rollback as an option - only focuses on completing the upgrade forward

assertions.expectation: Explains that retrying is usually better than rollback (rolling back means creating a new pool at old version)
assertions.reason: Response doesn't discuss rollback at all or explain why retry is preferable to rollback

assertions.expectation: Addresses the remaining 2 node pools: they can still be upgraded once the failed pool issue is resolved
assertions.reason: Response explicitly addresses the remaining pools in 'Post-Recovery Steps' section with commands to upgrade them after fixing the main pool

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to diagnose the failure (operation details, node status, pod events)
assertions.reason: Response includes multiple diagnostic commands: kubectl get nodes, kubectl describe nodes, kubectl get pods for checking status and events
